reviews.review_text: Had a drive through site which was easy to access. Staff are helpful. Some highway noise but not bad. Stayed 2 nights and was fine. Amenities clean but 70's style

A little road noise but all but goes away overnight.  Sites can be on the small side but you can manage.  Toilets and showers were sparkling clean. Looks like it is being done back up. The only van park in town. Water taps should be run before connecting as looks like some have had little use and a bit brown for few few litres, but fine after that. Water was very soft a nice change from the coast.
Poor offering - runs down facilities, wifi provided but did not work.

Horrible.  No light at night. Hard to find.  No signage to rv area.  Very dangerous at night.  Cabin very old.  Curtains don't close so no privacy. Awful  experience.  Bathroom old and horrible. Won't stay here again.  Paid $125 for the night and feel really disappointed 😞
Great little park just outside of the main town, easy access to everything. Staff were super friendly and the park was kept well maintained and clean!

I stayed on a powered site a few months ago, just a few days after the lovely new owners took over. Even though I arrived a few minutes after they'd closed, they opened up and found me a great site for my van. The amenities were lovely and clean, and heated (Cooma was cold at night, even in summer). The best option I could find in town with a dog (I'd booked into Cooma High Country Motel but didn't want to stay there). Some folks mention noise, and I'm a light sleeper, but I couldn't hear any road noise where I was. Great place to stop, highly recommended.

we stayed on a powered site a little further up and had a good night. The street noise could also be heard there, but much less.
Nice ameneties. The staff were very friendly, especially the lady on the reception! Thank you very much.
We booked into a Mountain View cabin, and it looked like it had recently been refurbished. It was lovely, clean and bright, and very comfortable. We would definitely stay here again.

I was camping 🏕️ during my stay and found everything a camper needs during a short break in a cycling tour. The amenities where well kept and clean. But what I liked the most was the team of people working there, very pleasant and chatty

Very happy with our 4 night stay ! Great site and the amenities were clean ! Staff were fantastic ! It wasn’t a problem to stay 2 extra nights when our car needed major repairs from the local mechanic!
Old but clean. Receptionist was lovely. Price was cheaper than others we stayed at.

Stayed on a powered site for a good week in March 2026.
Great holiday Park which is under renovation or upgrade after new management took over. Friendly staff and locals.
Park is well kept and close to the ski fields and the mountains in general.
Well worth a stay but be careful, a speed camera car sat outside the park most days at different times of the day ready to snap you up.

Extremely helpful, I had to get some repairs to my car and the owner said not to stress about check out time.  Amenities were extremely clean and tidy.  The site for our van was large are relatively easy to back into.  We used the camp kitchen, also very clean and friendly.  Easy walk into town and lastly a beautiful golden retriever.

This is not really within walking distance to town.
We booked a drive through site and when we went to check in they said that the floods had caused the ground to become uneven and therefore the site was unusable (I think site 31 or 32). We were offered another drive through site (81) but it was so unlevel that we had to uncouple the van to make the caravan level! So was really not worth paying extra for a drive through!
There is no other choice in cooma for vans, so just be careful booking and paying extra for a drive through.
Very helpful staff. Amenities clean. Close to town and attractions.

We booked for. Group of 15 vans and asked for us to be be together or close to. We were spread all over the park.
The sites were too small for the vans and had pot holes everywhere. It took some of our people ages to even get their vans into the sites and some hung over the edges of the walls.
The park really does need major renovations. Amenities were clean. I wouldn’t recommend for large groups

Friendly park but needs a lot of work to tidy it up. Rough  roads to get in and some very tight spots to access. We have a large van so difficult to access many sites
Park needs some attention to tidy it up
Great spot to stay. The staff are friendly.
Had a powered pitch. Good facilities.
Great little park close to snow field, this is the place to stay , great little town of Cooma, very clean amenities and good sites
Handy to the highway and excellent friendly service
Clean, tidy, relaxed place, great place for powered-site camping, with BBQ’s, camp kitchen, enormous place with lots of birds to watch
Set up at a powered site in my campervan. Nice shady spot, nice quiet park and facilities were very clean.

We recently stayed at Cooma Holiday Park after experiencing some car troubles. The ladies in the office, Dieu and Amalee were wonderful and so accommodating trying to help us as our length of stay was unknown .
Dee and her partner are the new owners and it’s evident they are putting in quite a bit of TLC and making improvements into the once tired park.
We wish them well for the future.. 🙏🏻
We stayed at the back on no power site
Clean and tidy cabins. Excellent service from the owners. Reasonable prices.
I first visited in 1974 and have always enjoyed staying at Cooma Holiday park (Snowtells) Thank you for another great visit.
Good clean park ,friendly staff
Great quiet park close to the mountains and high country

The shower was fantastic. Large cubical with plenty of space. Perhaps not enough hangers. Not really and shade for tents. The camp kitchen is functional but small and neglected. Actually the whole camp has an overall sense of being run down and having seen better days. It is the only caravan park in town. The person who checked is in was very friendly and helpful. The wifi works which is great. Lots of places that advertise wifi don't really have something that works so this place is great. I would very happily stay here again but would not drive out of my way to do it.
Little run down but nice bones. Enjoyable stay

Our cabin was very unclean there were empty cans in the bbq some guests found cigarette butts in their cabins only one roll of toilet paper and one garbage bag for 200 a night their are far better options in Cooma.. stay at your own risk would not recommend
Had caravan site, staff helpful, amenities clean and tidy, park is a bit dated but good access to sites

Great location and friendly staff. Check in no hassle.
Stay at a non-powered tent site. Roads in need a lot of work as badly eroded. Sites are level but ground is very hard (bring sturdy pegs and hammer!). Water nearby and camp kitchen close but very basic. Amenities were very clean and lots of hot water. Fire ring available (in season) and wood can be bought at office.
Generally the park needs a good refresh and bought up to current standards. Road is noisy but not too bad.
Very quiet, well maintained, amenities very clean.
Always going there every year for last 6-7 years with large group of our hiking group Sydhikers. Very good price and the cabins are warm and cosy.
Great spot for a campervan lots of wildlife ,possums took my apple 🍎 then sat on my grandson shoe
Very Good
Bit rundown but clean.
Cheap stay. Pretty old and in need of repairs. Good heating. Mini golf course is unique

Excellent! This camping ground and hostel are truly impressive. The staff are incredibly enthusiastic, answering all my questions with a very friendly attitude. What's more, the prices here are very competitive compared to the market. I am very satisfied with my experience here and will definitely recommend it to my friends.
This park is situated on a bit of a hill so not always guaranteed a flat caravan site.  Nice overnight stay but cold.  All facilities available and they were clean.

Hi, I'm shermyra I did stay in your shereer cabins going back 5-years ago when I was with my partner Rudolf sudy. Sadly, my partner died last year on my b.day. hi, Kristen@coomatouristpark. Yes did enquire about tent-site accommodation.

It is older but very clean generous drive through site  Helpful staff. We were warned that it was going to be -5 degrees and suggested that we disconnect our hose over night and fill the kettle. Also ensured we knew about and had alpine diesel. Great advice taps did freeze. Amenities were in good order and very clean. Great hot water. Good value would go again.
Stayed in caravan on a site, arrived late. Staff were great. Toilet and shower facilities are very clean. Great drive thru sites
Grwat place too kick of your visit to the snow fields and just as good for summer excursion thru the Kosciusko national park
Average at best! Very dated

The worst kept caravan park we have ever been to. Booked a powered site with water but it had no tap! Asked to be moved to another site which also had no tap! Was told by the person in the office that it must be there - umm, it wasn’t. Had to run the hose from another site - lucky we had a long hose.
Rubbish and weeds everywhere, broken bbq’s - burners corroded and rotten. Lots of truck noise too as it’s close to a main road. The entire park is in total disrepair. Incredibly the shower/toilet block which was oldish was clean and the shower was good. Fortunately we were only there one night!
Right on the main road so very noisy

Went for easter long weekend as a part of exploring Kosciusko NP. Stayed there for two days. It was perfect for its price.

When we checked in they provided the map of the venue to locate our cabin and all other amenities. So its quick and easy.

Very nice location in the bush. Lots of pesky possums and kangaroos. Very patchy Telstra reception but the park has pay as you go wifi which is adequate.  Because of heaps of tall trees Starlink would be very marginal I’m thinking.  The Kiosk sells firewood and kindling.  A lot of the caravan sites are drive through so it’s easy to setup.  All sites have fireplaces.  The ablutions block was very nice inside even though it looks crap from the outside.  It’s an easy drive back to Jindabyne for supplies.
Great little Aussie gem.

Check in lady was friendly, but in a hurry so I felt I couldn't ask questions about the area. The unpowered site at the top was slushy and dirty - someone must have been either watering the grass or has used a hose up there and saturated the area. This water also wet part of the sites below. There were discarded car mats and used cable ties everywhere as well as plenty of litter. The ladies shower block only had one light working. A real disappointment at the end of a long traveling day. Although not the park's fault the relentless barking of the dog next door made for an unpleasant stay.
Run down, unmaintained, pot holes in roads and on sites, no lightning to see way to amenities, it's a shocker.

This is an older park and hasn't been really well maintained. The toilets were clean but are very dated with a chain for the flush mechanism. The power boxes and taps are not really adequate for the allocated spots. The sites are a good size and the service at the office was excellent. The park is positioned well on the outskirts of town, it is a short drive to the main centre.
Long plain but free camp good
Not special but no complaints

Liegt direkt an der Hauptstraße, tagsüber laut aber nachts hat man nichts gehört.
Die Beleuchtung war sehr spärlich, die Toilette und Dusche im Haus mit Codierung bedürfen der Renovierung, aber es war alles sauber.
Für ein/zwei Nächte ist es ok. Guter Ausgangspunkt für den Besuch der Snowy Mountains.
Wir waren auf dem Campground, zu den Häusern kann ich nichts sagen.
Empfang war freundlich.
Good quiet caravan park, nothing fancy but great value for money, and one of the few parks who doesn't charge you an arm and a leg for an unpowered overnight stay. Would definitely stay again.
Great experience looking how the Snowy was built. Food was lovely in the cafeteria

The ensuite cabin we stayed in, while somewhat dated, was neat and tidy. The beds were supremely comfortable, and the shower was nice and hot. Location wise, the park is fairly close to town but still not really what I'd call walkable. Free wifi is available, and it was quite fast for caravan park wifi.

We stayed in our own van overnight on a Saturday. The lady who booked us in was really lovely. The reviews here about cleanliness are a little unfair. We found the facilities to be clean. Yes the place is tired and needs some work on paint and grounds and signs. I had very low expectations due to the reviews.
Yes it’s an older park. It is located conveniently walk distance to the alpine hotel and a great cafe called the Loft.
We are self contained so don’t need a camp kitchen or other facilities just wanted power and water. If you need to stay in Cooma we believe it’s fine here- just don’t expect it to be perfect and pimped. It’s definitely not that but it was quiet and had all the basics. For a couple like us it was fine.
Nice shade park right on the river clean toilets and showers
Great and affordable to stop for a shower during a remote camping trip.

My Experience not worth the money you pay  very untidy not well looked after ,Toilet  not keep very clean nor showers or laundry   Code given to just about everybody in town (eg showers laundry (not good idea) campers only  . Come on snowy mountain council you have a good little place clean it Up  just like Bombala c/van park tops excellent !!!!!

Very disappointed. Run down shabby facilities, though toilet block has recently been repainted which is a plus. Camp kitchen extremely limited to a cold water sink and 2 BBQS. Walking paths to amenities are not in best condition so a bit hazardous in the dark. Sites are cramped and water outlet in an awkward position so hard to hook into.  The waste water from our neighbour was seeping into our site. Wouldn’t stay again

Shearers Quarters have everything needed for a short or extended stay. Up the back of the park away from traffic noise however did not appreciate the loud mouth very rude male swearing at us in the seperate shearing quarter in front row. A definite put off if his a long term resident stay away.

This place helped my mum out so much when her car broke down...
The lady on the phone was the lovely. She helped mum (mum is 74 y.o.) like she was her own mum.
So happy that mum pulled up at this place when she broke down....
6 Stars if I could

We’ve stayed at many nice parks however this was not one of them. I expected better for $51 and I guess that they can charge what they like due to only park in town. Our site was right by the bins and foot traffic could look straight in to our van. Toilet cistern leaked water on you when doing your biz and the toilet roll holder located to the back of the side wall added to my frustration here. We would have stayed longer in the town had the park been better.

A great, quiet caravan park with lots of room. Facilities are good, it's only a minute drive into town and the staff are helpful.
Powered sites are a good size, easy to have a tent, car and space to move.
Oldie but a goodie

The Cooma Snowy Moujntains Tourist Park is very run down and would have to be one of the worst caravan parks we have stayed in.  The amenities were not cleaned nor tidied during our three night stay and it appears very little, if any, maintenance is down around the park.  Very dissapointing to see a park in such a nice location be so untidy.

Didn't enjoy this park at all. At this time of the year, everything is dead. It's cold, so that adds to the dreariness of the place. I found it depressing.
Amenities were old but clean, and water was hot, thankfully.
Not for me.
Hopefully it looks better in spring and summer
Clean facilities. Fire pit. Spacious caravan space.
Needs some TLC
Very clean amenities, nice hot showers. Park is a bit dated but OK.

Lovely but old caravan park. Newish shower/toilet block is ok, but old block is in need of some revamp with no hot water available when we were there.|Staff were lovely and helpful. Everything was clean.|Place just needs a bit of love and spruce up.

Great park, very friendly owner and very accommodating for large parties regarding arrangements. Good clean amenities and beautiful (recently renovated) cabin.

We stayed in the unpowered section which back onto bush and is quite pretty, but is quite lumpy for access so won't suit some low caravans.

Good kitchen and games room for gathering and fire pits at several locations.
Nice country caravan park , bit tight on some roads and some amenities looking a bit tied , walk to town
not far away from the mountain. site is not the best but affordable during high season
We stayed in a motorhome, my only criticism is the sites are small. Otherwise amenities clean and tidy, management friendly and helpful. Pet friendly and quiet very close to town

Amenities are ok, great shower, park needs a lot of attention, run down , just needs a bit of tlc, almost empty but management parked a huge rig beside our little 18footer blocking the lovely afternoon sun , spread the love over the entire park management.

A tired run down place to avoid. Welcomed by faded signage and potholed driveway. Dead lawns and unkept edging. Twisted taps that have been backed into. I found the ablution block but first thought it was an abandoned building with dust and weeds and cobwebs and litter. It is clean inside!!! the camp kitchen again looked tired. A pile of charcoal signified a previous camp fire on the lawn. But it was months ago as the grass was growing through it.  the place gets plenty of custom and they charge top dollar so no excuses.

This place is so tired. The roads are broken up and potholed  the lawns are dead. The taps bent and pipes rusted  the paths to the ablution block are dusty littered and overgrown. Everything needs a coat of paint. The ablution block is clean inside. It is very busy and the charges not low so they have no excuse for poor presentation and upkeep

Nice quiet spot. A bit older but well looked after facilities. Fairly close to town, and there's a great lookout nearby at Mt Gladstone that is very picturesque at sunset. Mt Glastone is also a mountain bike park which is a short ride from the caravan park.
Very friendly people
Such a lovely friendly place
We were there for only one night. The facilities were clean. The park has been awesome in its day. Great for families, it has a mini golf course.

Room was spacious, unfortunately we were in a family cabin that has a dead tree in the verandah, the bed was low but comfortable, lounge was uncomfortable, good kitchen, some of the windows didn't have flyscreen so couldn't open then due to bugs

In the bedroom there were two windows but we couldn't open either as one of the flyscreen was screwed closed as and the other has none

Needs a bit of maintenance lots of pot holes
Basic camp ground and caravan park, got all the services you need, game room, basic kitchen and super clean amenities. Walking distance to town shopping and coffee shops.
Can't comment on rooms as we stayed in our Kombi.  Manager very friendly and helpful.  Park nice and close to main centre.  Very quiet, which is great.  Good amenities.
Bit tired but everything seems to work.

No heating worked in our accomodation and as it was -3 deg its rather an important commodity, very dirty,dog poo lying around ground and price $165 and night for a caravan like accomodation was extremely expensive.
Stayed here for one night on a powered sight. It's a big park, with toilets and showers and a laundry with dryers. Good location, not far from town. Enjoyed our stay!
Unfortunately a bit rundown need to spend money and tidy it up. Could be a good camp.
Superb place! The amenities and other parts are super convenient, which made the stay a lot more memorable! Furthermore, staff was super nice and helpful!
Unpowered camping spots are close together but large enough for a car and 4 swags and fire. Tidy and clean facilities, great staff, nice gardens.
Very convenient place with awesome facilities and amenities

Our car broke down just outside of Cooma and we rang to see if we could get a site. We were very grateful to the manager who opened up the office to check us in after hours. A lovely park with well maintained facilities.
I'm after simple, great showers. Nice people and amenities. This place had it all. Completely happy would easily stay again.

We had an ensuite cabin. Maintenance was very poor - cabinets all chipped, bathroom sink so old the white surface had been rubbed off, two lights in the bedroom didn't work, extractor fan light didn't  work and sounded like it was about to die. Wall heater didn't work either but there was a mobile one. Overall the heating was insufficient.

We stayed at this place for one night on a powered site we were lucky there was a room available for us the price was great no crazy offloaded ski season prices Staff for accommodating at a fish and chip where van at the front which was good for an emergency meal everything was clean usable friendly and it was a good place to stop for a quick night, The showers toilets were clean it was places to wash your clothes and dry them has minigolf and activities for the kids like a playroom. If you’re travelling up to Jindabyne I would recommend staying at this place and supporting this country caravan park as it’s not too far from Jindabyne or Thredbo and is definitely much better value option for the amenities
Excellent service and friendly staff

For a stay in walking distance to town this is a great spot. A shame the park is shabby and rundown. But staying and exploring the Cooma town and region was very enjoyable. Stayed in self sufficient van but the amenities were very tidy and clean when I viewed them. Has options for firepits which is good. Some road noise but wasn't an issue. Can recommend just don't have high expectations. It serves a purpose.
